description_long: This object is a large, roughly circular stone block with a wide, hollow center, resembling a ring or a natural stone arch. The upper surface is mostly flat with irregular, organic edges and is covered in patches of green moss or lichen, suggesting exposure to the elements. The stone is thick and appears to be quite heavy, with a weathered, uneven texture and visible chipping along the rim. The underside is darker and more shadowed, with the block supported by several thick, irregular stone legs or columns, creating an open space beneath. The coloration is predominantly a light grayish-tan with green highlights, giving it a natural, aged appearance. This structure could serve as a decorative or functional barrier in a landscaped urban park or garden, possibly as a boundary marker or ornamental feature.
description: A large, ring-shaped stone block with a hollow center, weathered surface, and patches of green moss, supported by thick stone legs.
description_view_0: The stone block is seen from a slightly elevated angle, showing the hollow center and the uneven, mossy rim with three visible supporting legs.
description_view_1: This view highlights the flatness of the top surface and the irregular, organic edge, with more of the mossy texture visible along the rim.
description_view_2: The block's circular shape and the thickness of the stone are prominent, with the hollow center and supporting legs clearly visible.
description_view_3: The moss coverage on the rim is most apparent, and the open space beneath the block is well defined, showing the structure's support.
attribute_location_context: urban park landscaping or decorative garden feature
attribute_installation_method: set directly on compacted soil or gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible moss growth on the upper edges and some surface roughness, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure and weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely placed as a standalone feature
